apps.name: Property Manager V1
apps.summary: Maintenance command center for small landlords: intake, dispatch, updates, and issue history.
apps.reviewGate: Jeff verdict: fail, but with usable bones. The blocking issue is trust: the app can present convincing seed/demo reads when the DB is missing while real write flows are effectively broken.
apps.nextStep: Fix the truth problem first: either require a working DB or surface an unavoidable demo/read-only banner. Then run a real DB-backed walkthrough with verified writes, resolve the property/unit-creation goal mismatch, clean the server-action warning, and add explicit disconnected-data states.
notes: Mario: the right wedge is a maintenance command center, not an all-in-one property management suite.
Barry Bot: Core multi-role workflow is live: operator flows, tenant submission/timeline/photos, vendor dispatch/queue/detail, and credential-based auth are in place.

Barry Bot: Current gate is no longer the auth-boundary harness; Bob fixed the hard-coded-port timeout issue in commit 06a6e79 and the teardown hang in commit aac70e0, so auth regression coverage now passes and exits cleanly.
Barry Bot: Product direction is now cleaner: operator pays, tenant/vendor access is free, invite-based onboarding is the path, and regions/urgency/expected completion/import-export are in scope.

Barry Bot: Latest signal: Jeff reviewed the walkthrough and failed sign-off despite coherent UI/read-only structure. Core issue is silent seed/demo fallback when DATABASE_URL is missing: the app can look healthy while writes fail. Jeff also flagged missing property/unit creation UI versus the walkthrough goal, a visible server-action form warning, thin auth confidence in local fallback mode, and the need for explicit disconnected-data/error states before credibility returns.
